fresh_facts.claim: US B-1 strategic bombers took off from UK bases on March 11 to conduct strikes in Iran, marking their first reported use in the conflict.
The US deployed a third aircraft carrier, USS George H. W. Bush, to the Middle East region on March 7.
The US approved a $151 million arms sale to Israel on March 7.
US and Israeli air strikes targeted 26 of Iran's 31 provinces, including air defenses, ballistic missiles, IRGC bases, leadership facilities, and nuclear sites.
Tehran was the most heavily hit location in the US-Israeli air campaign.
Iranian missile launch rates have declined 90-92% and drone rates have declined 80-92% as of March 10.
A senior IRGC commander stated on March 10 that Iran is using only missiles with 1,000 kg or larger payloads and described ongoing attacks as the war's most intense.
The IRGC ordered evacuation of Mariwan on March 3 amid ongoing strikes.
Ships were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz on March 11 as Iran escalated naval operations.
US CENTCOM destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers near the Strait of Hormuz on March 10.
Since February 28, the US and Israel have conducted hundreds of strikes against Iran, while Iran has launched over 500 missiles and 2,000 drones by March 5.
The US sank 17 Iranian warships during the campaign.
Strikes on IRGC bases and internal security installations, including detention facilities in Mariwan, led to a jailbreak and stretched regime control in Kurdish areas.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died during operations referred to as 'Operation Epic Fury.'
Following Khamenei's death, Iran reportedly formed a three-person leadership council with his son identified as a potential successor.
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt addressed succession questions regarding Iran on March 4.
The National Council of Resistance of Iran (NCRI) announced a provisional government based on a Ten-Point Plan for democratic transition.
International figures including David Alton in London welcomed the NCRI's provisional government announcement.
Resistance Units inside Iran echoed support for the NCRI's provisional government plan.
Recent protests in Iran stemmed from economic crisis and war threats and were violently suppressed.
Iran's regime is relying on coercion rather than consent to maintain control amid political limbo.
US and Israeli forces degraded Iranian air defenses over western Iran and Tehran as of March 2.

regime_fall.current_status: US and Israeli forces have expanded strikes to 26 of Iran's 31 provinces with B-1 bombers now engaged, while Iran's offensive capacity has collapsed by 80-92% and a third US carrier deploys to the region. Mojtaba Khamenei's succession remains contested amid an internet blackout, fuel shortages, IRGC demoralization, and emerging alternative governance proposals from the NCRI.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US B-1 strategic bombers conducted strikes on March 11 marking their first reported engagement, while coordinated US-Israeli air campaign expanded to 26 Iranian provinces including targeting of air defenses, ballistic missiles, IRGC bases, leadership facilities, and nuclear sites, with Tehran as the most heavily hit location.
